title: Get rules by ID
content:
Use this endpoint to get rules by rule IDs. ## Comparing v1.2 and v1.3 ```xtable |Changes{30%}|v1.2{35%}|v1.3{35%}| |---|---|---| |Endpoint path| /v1.2/optimizer/rule/get/| /v1.3/optimizer/rule/get/| |Request parameter data type |`advertiser_id`: number
`rule_ids`: number[]|`advertiser_id`: string
`rule_ids`: string[]| |Response parameter data type|`dimension_ids`: number[]
`task_exec_id`: number
`rule_id`: number|`dimension_ids`: string[]
`task_exec_id`: string
`rule_id`: string| |Response parameter deprecated in v1.3 |/|`recipients`| ``` ## Request **Endpoint** **Method** GET **Header** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |Access-Token {required}|string|Authorized access token. For details, see [Authentication](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1738373164380162).| |Content-Type {required}|string|Request message type
Allowed format: `"application/json"`. | ``` **Parameters** ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |advertiser_id {required}|string| Advertiser ID | |rule_ids {required}|string[]| IDs of the rules that you want to get| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code JSON JSON) { "advertiser_id": "75960823018", "rule_ids":["6982553361971232769","6984697088064290818"] } (/code) ``` ## Response ```xtable |Field|Data Type|Description| |---|---|---| |code |number|Response code. For the complete list of response codes and descriptions, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |message |string|Response message. For details, see [Appendix - Return Codes](https://ads.tiktok.com/marketing_api/docs?id=1737172488964097).| |request_id|string| Unique ID of the request.| |data|object|Returned data.| #|page_info|object| Pagination information.| ##|page|number| Current page number.| ##|page_size|number|Page size. | ##|total_number|number|Total number of results. | ##|total_page|number| Total pages of results.| #|rules|object[]| List of rules| ##|apply_objects|object[]| Objects that you want to apply the rules to. Objects can be campaigns, ad groups, or ads.| ###|dimesion |string| Category of the object. Enum values: `CAMPAIGN`, `ADGROUP`, `AD`.| ###|dimension_ids| string[]| IDs of the objects| ###|pre_condition_type |string| Conditions to filter out objects that you do not want to include.
  • `SELECTED`: Only include those that are selected by IDs.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_CAMPAIGN`: Select all active campaigns.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD_GROUP`: Select all active ad groups.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD`: Select all active ads.
  • ` ALL_ACTIVE_AD_GROUP_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all active ad groups under the selected campaign.
  • `ALL_ACTIVE_AD_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all active ads under selected dimensions
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_CAMPAIGN`: Select all inactive campaigns.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_GROUP`: Select all inactive ad groups
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD`: Select all inactive ads.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_GROUP_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all inactive ad groups under the selected campaign.
  • `ALL_INACTIVE_AD_UNDER_SELECTED`: Select all inactive ads under the selected campaign or ad group (specified by `dimension` and `dimension_ids`)
| ###|bind_type|string|Bind type of the rule. Enum values: `BIND`, `UNBIND`.| ##|conditions|object[]| Conditions| ###|subject_type|string| Subjects that the condition will be based on. Enum values:
  • `COST`
  • `DAILY_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE`
  • `LIFETIME_BUDGET_SPENDING_RATE`
  • `IMPRESSION`
  • `CLICK`
  • `CONVERSION`
  • `CONVERSION_SKAN`
  • `CVR`
  • `CVR_SKAN`
  • `CPA`
  • `CPM`
  • `CPC`
  • `CTR`
  • `COST_PER_RESULT`
  • `RESULT`
  • `RESULT_RATE`
  • `BID_STRATEGY`
  • `OPTIMIZATION_GOAL`
  • `OBJECTIVE`
  • `NAME`
  • `DAYS_SINCE_CREATION`
  • `NO_CONDITION`
  • `ROAS_PURCHASE`
  • `ROAS_IMPRESSION_ADS`
  • `ROAS_PURCHASE_SKAN`
  • `ROAS_COMPLETE_PAYMENT`
  • `ROAS_PURCHASES_ONSITE`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CPA`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CONVERSION`
  • `NOT_REAL_TIME_CVR`
| ###|range_type |string| Time range for the condition. Enum values:
  • `TODAY`
  • `YESTERDAY`
  • `PAST_THREE_DAYS`
  • `PAST_FIVE_DAYS`
  • `PAST_SEVEN_DAYS`
  • `LIFETIME`
| ###|match_type|string| Operator to connect a condition with a value. Enum values:
  • `GT` (greater than)
  • `LT` (less than)
  • `BETWEEN`
  • `MATCH`
  • `CONTAINS`
  • `NOT_CONTAINS`
  • `START_WITH`
  • `END_WITH`
  • `STRING_EQUAL` | ###|values |string[]| Values for the condition.| ###|calculation_type|string|Enum values:
  • `ALL_OBJECTS`
  • `OF_EACH_OBJECT`
    • | ##|action |object[]| Actions to be taken when the condition is met| ###|subject_type|string| Type of actions to take. Enum: `TURN_ON`, `TURN_OFF`, `MESSAGE`, `DAILY_BUDGET`, `LIFERIME_BUDGET`, `BID`| ###|action_type|string| Enum values: `INCREASE`, `DECREASE`, `ADJUST_TO`| ###|value_type|string| Enum values: `EXACT`, `PERCENT`| ###|value|object|
    • When `action_type` is `INCREASE` or `DECREASE`, users can specify the maximum(if `INCREASE`) or minimum(if `DECREASE`) budget using `use_limit` and `limit`.
    • When `action_type` is `ADJUST_TO`, no need to provide `use_limit` and `limit`.| ###|frequency_info|object| Frequency settings. It can be used when the action `subject_type` is `DAILY_BUDGET`, `LIFERIME_BUDGET`, or `BID`| ####|type|string| Enum:
      • `ONLY_ONCE`
      • `ONCE_IN_24_H`
      • `ONCE_IN_48_H`
      • `ONCE_IN_1_W`
      • `CUSTOM`
      | ####|custom_frequency_type|string|Custom frequency type. Valid only when `type` is `CUSTOM`. Enum:
      • `N_MINTUE_Y_TIMES`
      • `N_HOUR_Y_TIMES`
      • `N_DAY_Y_TIMES`
      | ####|time|number|It is the `N` in `custom_frequency_type`| ####|count|number|It is the `Y` in `custom_frequency_type`| ##|notification |object| Notification settings| ###|notification_type | string|Notification type you need. Enum values:
      • `TASK_FINISH`: Notification will be sent when the task is finished. This option is only for `MESSAGE` subject type.
      • `ANY_CHANGES`: If the `subject_type` for the action is not `MESSAGE` but you still want to receive emails, use this option.
      • `NOT_NOTIFICATION`: No notifications are needed.
      | ###|email_setting|object|Email notification settings| ####|notification_period|string|Enum values:
      • `EVERY_TIME`
      • `TIME_SCHEDULE`: notifications will be sent together around a specified time.
      | ####|email_exec_time|string[]|Required when `notification_period` is`TIME_SCHEDULE`. Notification email sent time. For example, "12:00".| ####|no_result_notification|boolean|It will be set as false by default if not provided, meaning no notification will be sent if there is no result.| ##|rule_exec_info |object| Rule run settings| ###|exec_time_type|string| When the rule will be run. Enum values:
      • `PER_HALF_HOUR`: the rule will be run every half hour.
      • `CUSTOM`: the rule will be run at a customized time (specified in the `exec_time` field).
      • `HALF_HOUR_IN_SPECIFIC_TIME_PERIOD`: the rule will be run every 30 minutes during specified time range on specifed days during the week
      • `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`: the rule will be run only once at a customized time (specified in the `exec_time` field).
      | ###|exec_time|string| If `exec_time_type` is set to `CUSTOM` or `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`, use this field to specify the exact time when the rule will be run.
      Format:
      • "HH:MM" when `exec_time_type` is `CUSTOM`. For example: "12:00".
      • "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM" when `exec_time_type` is `SPECIFIC_TIME_ACCURATE_ONCE`. For example: "2023-04-20 04:20".
      | ###|time_period_info|object[]|Valid when `exec_time_type` is `HALF_HOUR_IN_SPECIFIC_TIME_PERIOD`. It has `start_time`, `end_time`, `date_type` and `num` to specify the time period information.| ####|num|integers[]| Each number in the list represents the day in the week or month.
      • When `date_type` is `WEEK`, value range for this field is [1, 7], meaning Monday to Sunday.
      • When `date_type` is `MONTH`, value range for this field is [1, 28], meaning 1st day to the 28th day of the month.
      | ####|start_time|string| Start time of this time period. For example, "12:00".| ####|end_time|string| End time of this time period. For example, "12:00".| ####|date_type|string| Type of date. Enum values: `DAY`, `WEEK`, `MONTH`.| ##|last_check_result_summary|object| Information about the last rule run| ###|check_datetime|string| Date and time when the rule was checked, in UTC+0 time zone| ###|exec_datetime|string| Date and time when the rule was run, in UTC+0 time zone| ###|change_success|number| Objects that have been changed| ###|no_change|number| Objects that have not been changed| ###|change_fail|number| Objects that failed to be changed| ###|task_exec_id|string| Rule execution ID| ##|rule_id|string|ID of the rule| ##|name |string| Name of the rule| ##|rule_status|string|Rule status. Enum values:
      • `ON`: on.
      • `OFF`: off.
      • `DELETED`: deleted.
      | ##|create_datetime|string|Date and time when the rule was created, in UTC+0 time zone| ``` ### Example ```xcodeblock (code JSON JSON) { "code": 0, "message": "OK", "request_id": "202107290652080102450151363E025325", "data": { "rules": [ { "rule_exec_info": { "exec_time": "", "exec_time_type": "PER_HALF_HOUR" }, "rule_status": "ON", "name": "[API]Automated Rule--example-update", "notification": { "notification_type": "TASK_FINISH" }, "rule_id": "6984697088064290818", "create_datetime": "2021-07-29 06:45:04", "actions": [ { "subject_type": "MESSAGE" } ], "last_check_result_summary": { "change_fail": 0, "check_datetime": "0001-01-01 00:00:00", "change_success": 0, "task_exec_id": "0", "not_change": 0, "exec_datetime": "0001-01-01 00:00:00" }, "apply_objects": [ { "dimension_ids": [ "1694011873852465" ], "dimension": "CAMPAIGN", "bind_type": "BIND", "pre_condition_type": "SELECTED" } ], "conditions": [ { "subject_type": "CLICK", "values": [ "1" ], "match_type": "GT", "range_type": "LIFETIME" } ] } ], "page_info": { "total_page": 1, "page_size": 10, "total_number": 1, "page": 1 } } } (/code) ```
